chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P No. D – 294 of 2025 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Fresh case 1. For orders on office objection at flag ‘A’ 2. For orders on CMA No.1327/2025 (Ex.A) 3. For hearing of main case. 20-10-2025 Mr. Mansoor Ali Panhwar, Advocate for the Petitioners >>>>>..<<<<< On 30-09-2025, the following order was passed;- “None present for the Petitioners nor any intimation is received; same was the position on the last date of hearing. Under the circumstances, this Petition is liable to be dismissed for non- prosecution; however, in the interest of justice, adjourned to 08- 10-2025. Let intimation notice be issued to the Counsel for the Petitioners for the said date. Counsel for the Petitioners is also directed to satisfy the Court about maintainability of this Petition as the Petitioners have old disability certificates, which are not issued by the Board constituted under the Act, 2018.” Upon perusal of the petition, it transpires that some of the petitioners do not possess disability certificates issued by the Medical Board constituted under the Sindh Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2018, by the Board established pursuant to the said Act. Furthermore, it appears that each Petitioner has a distinct cause of action, yet they have jointly sought relief through a single petition. In view of the above, the present Petition is disposed of with directions to the petitioners who have been issued disability certificates by the Medical Board constituted under the aforesaid Act may file separate petition(s) afresh, in accordance with their respective cause of action.

Furthermore, it appears that each Petitioner has a distinct cause of action, yet they have jointly sought relief through a single petition. In view of the above, the present Petition is disposed of with directions to the petitioners who have been issued disability certificates by the Medical Board constituted under the aforesaid Act may file separate petition(s) afresh, in accordance with their respective cause of action. However, the petitioners who do not currently possess disability certificates issued by the Medical Board are directed to approach the relevant authorities for issuance of the same and upon obtaining such certificates from the Medical Board constituted under the Act, they may approach the concerned forums for redressal of their grievances. Judge Judge ARBROHI/PS
